message:
feat(node): quarantine incident sample fixtures to test-only scope and add incident CLI e2e coverage

Rename sample_incident_events to fixture_incident_events and mark as
fixture-only with doc comments. Reclassify PSI-003 from
disallowed_live_shortcut to allowlisted_simulation now that the live
path uses --evidence-path instead of canned timelines.

Add incident_cli_e2e integration tests validating:
- explicit evidence-path writes a valid bundle with correct metadata
- missing evidence causes fail-closed nonzero exit with no bundle created

Update all contract specs (bd-2fqyv_4_1, bd-vll), governance inventory,
regression scanner rules/tests, replay bundle checker, and README CLI
examples to reflect the fixture rename and evidence-path flag.

Closes bd-2fqyv.4.3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -591,7 +591,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","title":"Implement real reproduction harness execution and explicit dry-run mode","description":"Implement the actual harness-backed verification flow and explicit dry-run/report-only mode. The dedicated validation bead will add the broad end-to-end matrix, schema assertions, and operator-facing logging checks.","design":"Normal mode should execute referenced checks; dry-run mode should produce planning/reporting output with unmistakable status markers. This bead should also emit the core status fields and execution hooks needed for downstream validation.","acceptance_criteria":"- Normal mode executes real verification work.
- Dry-run mode is explicit and never marked as pass.
- The implementation exposes stable status/logging hooks for follow-on validation.","notes":"The core requirement is truthfulness of outcome semantics, not just swapping in a different placeholder string.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.182821278Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:22.080182458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","placeholder-remediation","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.423645330Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:32.345874843Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","title":"Add reproduce-harness e2e scripts, failure-path coverage, and verbose execution logging","description":"Add the high-signal validation surface for `scripts/reproduce.py`: end-to-end scripts, failure-path coverage, and verbose execution logs that prove the harness actually ran or that the tool was intentionally in dry-run mode.","design":"Tests should cover successful execution, missing harness mapping, harness failure, and dry-run/report-only mode. Logging should capture the claim identifier, resolved harness target, command/result summary, and the explicit status distinction between executed verification and non-executing planning output.","acceptance_criteria":"- There are end-to-end scripts/tests for success, harness failure, missing mapping, and dry-run behavior.
- Output and logs make it impossible to confuse dry-run/reporting with a passing verification run.
- Machine-readable result schema is asserted in tests.
- User-facing docs/examples show how to interpret the detailed logs and statuses.","notes":"This bead protects operator trust in verification claims by making execution evidence inspectable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.543251023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:20.232973660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.543251023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.543251023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.11","title":"Harden DGIS no-barrier semantics instead of synthetic pass-through receipts","description":"Harden DGIS no-barrier behavior so the system no longer fabricates synthetic pass-through receipts when no matching barrier exists. The audit found `security/dgis/barrier_primitives.rs` building synthetic barriers/receipts with `passthrough-*` IDs in no-match cases.","design":"The system should distinguish clearly between:
- a real barrier check that passed
- a no-barrier / not-applicable condition
- a policy violation caused by missing required barrier coverage

A fabricated pass receipt blurs those categories and undermines auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"- No-barrier semantics are explicit and auditable.
- Synthetic pass-through receipt fabrication is removed or strictly limited to intentional non-live contexts.
- Callers/tests/audit codes reflect the new model.","notes":"This is a lower-confidence finding than the live CLI demo surfaces, but it is exactly the kind of audit shortcut that can erode trust in receipts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.735636861Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.928397068Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dgis","placeholder-remediation","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.978095298Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","title":"Define explicit DGIS no-barrier semantics and receipt model","description":"Define the correct semantics for no-barrier cases: explicit NotApplicable / NoBarrier receipt, fail-closed missing-coverage error, or another deliberate model that does not masquerade as a real barrier pass.","design":"Resolve the semantics first, then update event codes, receipt types, and caller expectations accordingly.","acceptance_criteria":"- No-barrier state model is explicit.
- Receipt/event implications are documented.
- Follow-on implementation scope is clear.","notes":"Preserve auditability: downstream readers should always be able to tell what actually happened.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.283697574Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:31.656518025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","dgis","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:31.656442103Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.520568299Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Receipt/event implications are documented.
- Follow-on implementation scope is clear.","notes":"Preserve auditability: downstream readers should always be able to tell what actually happened.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.283697574Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:19:16.197590991Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","dgis","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:31.656442103Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.520568299Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","title":"Implement explicit DGIS no-barrier behavior and caller updates","description":"Implement the chosen DGIS semantics and update barrier callers so no-match cases no longer emit fabricated pass-through receipts. Exhaustive receipt-matrix proof and audit-log assertions are handled in the validation bead.","design":"Update receipt construction, event code selection, and call-site expectations together so the semantics stay coherent. Emit the core receipt/log fields that the validation bead will later ratchet.","acceptance_criteria":"- Synthetic pass-through receipt fabrication is removed from the audited path.
- Callers consume the new semantics correctly.
- Core audit outputs are explicit enough for downstream validation to assert the distinctions.","notes":"Do not leave partial compatibility layers that preserve the old ambiguous behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.829338298Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:22.443827740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dgis","implementation","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.068300680Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:32.582489070Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","title":"Add DGIS receipt matrix tests and audit-log coverage for no-barrier semantics","description":"Add the validation matrix for DGIS no-barrier semantics so receipt behavior, caller handling, and audit logs stay precise over time. This bead should prove that no-match cases are no longer disguised as ordinary passes.","design":"Use targeted unit tests for receipt construction, integration tests for caller behavior, and audit-log assertions that differentiate pass, not-applicable/no-barrier, and missing-required-barrier outcomes. Logs and receipts should expose enough detail for downstream audit tooling to classify the event without heuristics.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit and integration tests cover pass, no-barrier/not-applicable, and missing-coverage cases.
- Receipts and audit logs encode the distinctions explicitly.
- No fabricated pass-through receipts survive on the audited path.
- Docs/specs/tests explain the new receipt matrix clearly enough for future maintainers.","notes":"This is where the branch becomes auditably correct instead of merely less suspicious.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:20.600079951Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.12","title":"Run the final placeholder-remediation ratchet and close the audit","description":"Close the remediation program with a final audit and ratchet. This branch exists so the effort ends in a clean, provable steady state rather than a pile of partially fixed branches with lingering temporary exceptions.","design":"Use the inventory/scanner work plus the completed remediation branches to:
- remove temporary allowlist entries that are no longer needed
- prove live surfaces are clean
- update docs/specs/contracts so the repository tells the truth about itself","acceptance_criteria":"- Final audit passes with the intended steady-state allowlist.
- No audited live-surface placeholder remains unresolved.
- Docs/specs/contracts summarize the new truthfully-implemented state.","notes":"This branch is the exit criterion for the whole program.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.375630906Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.928849521Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["governance","placeholder-remediation","ratchet"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.612354637Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.4","title":"Replace incident sample events with real incident event sourcing","description":"Replace `incident bundle` generation from hard-coded sample events with a real incident event source. The audit showed that `main.rs` calls `sample_incident_events(&args.id)` before generating replay bundles, which means the command emits plausible but synthetic evidence.","design":"Define a real incident evidence pipeline:
- authoritative source for incident events and state snapshots
- explicit missing-data behavior
- deterministic fixture-only path for tests/examples
- bundle integrity checks that operate on real inputs rather than canned timelines","acceptance_criteria":"- `incident bundle` no longer uses sample events in its live path.
- Missing or malformed incident data causes clear failure.
- Tests/docs are updated to distinguish fixture mode from real mode.","notes":"This is a forensic surface; fake evidence is worse than no evidence. Prefer a hard error over fabricated timelines.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:21.292933448Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.925247238Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","incident","placeholder-remediation","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:21.641835061Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.4.1","title":"Define authoritative incident evidence source and validation contract","description":"Define the authoritative source contract for incident events, related state snapshots, and any metadata needed for replay bundle generation.","design":"Specify where the command loads incident evidence from, how that source is validated, and how deterministic fixtures are kept separate for tests and examples.","acceptance_criteria":"- Incident input contract is explicit and documented.
- Required fields and validation rules are defined.
- Fixture-only source is separated from the live source.","notes":"Think in terms of chain-of-custody: origin, validation, and reproducibility of incident evidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:22.019871702Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T16:35:42.480102517Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T16:35:42.480074444Z","close_reason":"Defined authoritative incident evidence package, validation, and fixture-boundary contract in docs/specs/section_10_5/bd-2fqyv_4_1_contract.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","incident","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:24.838244886Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:22.326815825Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.4.2","title":"Wire incident bundle CLI to real evidence sources and fail closed when absent","description":"Rewire the `incident bundle` command so it reads real incident evidence, validates it, and only then emits a replay bundle. Remove the sample-event live path.","design":"Make the command behavior binary: real evidence in, real bundle out; missing/invalid evidence, explicit failure. Preserve deterministic bundle generation once valid inputs exist.","acceptance_criteria":"- `main.rs` no longer calls `sample_incident_events()` in the live command path.
- Command failure modes for absent or invalid evidence are covered.
- Bundle generation remains deterministic for identical real inputs.","notes":"Keep any remaining sample helpers out of the production import graph.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:22.689646773Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:33.687810633Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:33.687786749Z","close_reason":"Validated incident bundle authoritative evidence path, fail-closed behavior, and stable-toolchain compatibility under rch verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","incident","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:22.993470352Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:26.299350406Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","title":"Quarantine incident sample fixtures and refresh bundle docs/tests","description":"Preserve deterministic examples by moving sample incident timelines into clearly test/fixture-only helpers and refreshing docs/tests around the new live behavior.","design":"Use fixtures intentionally: great for tests, unacceptable for live operator commands. Make that boundary obvious in naming, module placement, and docs.","acceptance_criteria":"- Sample incident helpers are test/fixture-scoped.
- Unit and incident bundle CLI/e2e tests show how to supply real incident inputs.
- Bundle contract tests no longer rely on live-path canned timelines.
- Logging/artifacts distinguish fixture timelines from authoritative incident evidence.","notes":"This bead protects developer ergonomics while keeping the production path honest.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:23.367224223Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:11:52.679898847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","incident","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:23.666368232Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:26.585636346Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","title":"Quarantine incident sample fixtures and refresh bundle docs/tests","description":"Preserve deterministic examples by moving sample incident timelines into clearly test/fixture-only helpers and refreshing docs/tests around the new live behavior.","design":"Use fixtures intentionally: great for tests, unacceptable for live operator commands. Make that boundary obvious in naming, module placement, and docs.","acceptance_criteria":"- Sample incident helpers are test/fixture-scoped.
- Unit and incident bundle CLI/e2e tests show how to supply real incident inputs.
- Bundle contract tests no longer rely on live-path canned timelines.
- Logging/artifacts distinguish fixture timelines from authoritative incident evidence.","notes":"This bead protects developer ergonomics while keeping the production path honest.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:23.367224223Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:26:10.031207919Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T17:26:10.031177102Z","close_reason":"Renamed the incident timeline helper to fixture_incident_events, kept it scanner-confined to tests/fixtures, refreshed README/spec/inventory/scanner contracts, added incident_cli_e2e coverage for explicit evidence-path success and fail-closed missing evidence, and validated with file-scoped rustfmt plus python3 scripts/check_replay_bundle.py --json, python3 scripts/check_placeholder_surface_inventory.py --json, and python3 -m unittest tests.test_check_placeholder_surface_inventory; full rch cargo validation remains blocked by unrelated franken_engine compile errors (StdlibError::ArgumentTypeError in stdlib.rs and expectedLoss typo in runtime_decision_theory.rs) and unrelated clippy lint in crates/franken-node/src/control_plane/dpor_exploration.rs:217","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","incident","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:23.666368232Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:19:26.585636346Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.5","title":"Resolve control-plane service skeleton into a truthful transport surface","description":"Resolve the control-plane API surface so it no longer presents a skeleton as if it were a live service. The audit found that `api/service.rs` and `api/middleware.rs` explicitly describe a service skeleton, note the absence of a real transport boundary, and emit zeroed performance baselines.","design":"This branch must produce a truthful steady state, not just a renamed placeholder. That means either:
- implement the real transport/request-lifecycle/cancellation boundary, or
- explicitly quarantine non-live pieces so no operator-facing contract implies a running service that does not exist.

The branch should also eliminate fake perf baselines and replace them with measured or intentionally absent data.","acceptance_criteria":"- The service surface no longer claims more than it implements.
- Transport/lifecycle semantics are explicit.
- Performance baseline output is real or explicitly unavailable, never hard-coded zero placeholders.
- Related tests/docs/gates reflect the chosen architecture.","notes":"The core bug here is dishonesty in the surface contract. A truthful quarantine is acceptable as an interim step if it is explicit and removes any live-service illusion.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:37.918146281Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.925712215Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","control-plane","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:38.201553673Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.5.1","title":"Choose and document the authoritative control-plane service boundary","description":"Decide and document the authoritative architecture for the control-plane service boundary: real transport implementation versus explicit internal/catalog-only quarantine. This decision needs to be made deliberately before code is refactored.","design":"Evaluate the current route metadata, middleware chain, cancellation expectations, telemetry model, and packaging surface. Then choose the architecture that best aligns with the project's real goals rather than preserving the current misleading middle state.","acceptance_criteria":"- Chosen service-boundary strategy is explicit.
- Interim and final truthfulness rules are documented.
- Follow-on implementation beads have clear scope.","notes":"Do not treat the current skeleton as a sacred baseline. If the right answer is to narrow the surface before expanding it, say so explicitly.

Implementation progress: `api/service.rs` now explicitly declares the surface as an in-process catalog/dispatch boundary rather than a live transport service, and endpoint reports now expose unavailable performance baselines with provenance instead of fake `0.0` values. Companion architecture/governance docs were updated to match that truth model.

Validation status: file-scoped `rustfmt --check crates/franken-node/src/api/service.rs` passes, but full `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets --features extended-surfaces` and targeted `rch exec -- cargo test ... endpoint_report_generation` are currently blocked by an unrelated syntax error in `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/workload_policy_manifold.rs` plus unrelated `cargo fmt --check` drift elsewhere in the shared dirty worktree.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:38.564311121Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:59:03.253448475Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:59:03.253420543Z","close_reason":"Architecture chosen and truthfulness contract documented/implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","architecture","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.349913958Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:38.850395632Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.5.2","title":"Implement the chosen transport/lifecycle strategy and remove ambiguous skeleton behavior","description":"Implement the selected service-boundary strategy from the architecture bead: either a real transport/request lifecycle/cancellation surface or an explicit quarantine of non-live assembly/catalog behavior.","design":"Any implementation here must align route metadata, middleware behavior, and runtime lifecycle semantics. Remove ambiguous middle states where the code looks like a live service but lacks the actual boundary behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Service implementation matches the chosen architecture.
- Misleading skeleton-only behavior is removed from live surfaces.
- Integration tests cover the resulting transport/truthfulness model.","notes":"Coordinate carefully with existing integration tests and any workflow gates that currently mention the skeleton.

Implementation progress: `api/service.rs` now exposes an explicit in-process catalog/dispatch boundary, carries transport-boundary metadata in endpoint reports, and no longer presents the surface as a live listener-owned service. Companion docs in `docs/architecture/` and `docs/governance/` were updated to match the chosen quarantine semantics.

Validation blocker: full `rch` cargo validation is currently blocked by unrelated shared-worktree issues outside this bead (syntax error in sibling `franken_engine` and unrelated formatting drift elsewhere in `franken_node`).","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:39.188875002Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:59:03.309069567Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","implementation","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:39.463099905Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:46.143842323Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -64,8 +64,9 @@ franken-node trust card npm:@example/plugin
 # 7) Run with strict policy controls
